author: [DEEP] Digital Fists
content: Multi-species support with wide size range, NFT-ownership/progression, sticky Jettie movement are some unique selling points we already have. We have some thoughts/designs for more (not ready to share).  But the real answer is we are building an entire interconnected ecosystem/economy where the MMO is the end-goal. Our arena, racing, dogfighting game modes are stepping stones to refine combat, movement, vehicles, locomotion, and multiplayer foundations. We aren't trying to compete with Fortnite, Apex, Forza directly. We are offering instanced game mode now to engage our community. We don't plan to linger on these instanced game modes for years, we want to quickly move onto Open World Scope.

author: SW4GN3R [STAR]
content: Quickly in my mind is early next year, rather than refining arena/racing for another couple years. We have already started on open world with the Galia vertical slice and work on it continues in the background, but shifting focus would mean majority of our UE devs are working on open world scope which we hope to do.   Yes, we feel our efforts around multiplayer, arena, racing, and progression will increase the community. Judging our ability to grow by what's currently live with the current P2P experiences versus what we have cooking right now with dedicated server, matchmaking, progression, seasonal unlocks/challenges, new equipment slots, etc will make this more clear. Its coming.

And IMO, there's a good chance we will get some of those home runs along the way to building the ultimate MMORPG.

content:
I have a VERY STRANGE problem with the rental page:
If I connect with wallet A, I see a specific list of of 42 fleets that are available to borrow. I already wondered: Only 42 fleets? I can then reload the list as often as I want, the list doesn't change. But when I switch to wallet B, I suddenly see A COMPLETELY different list with fleets that I've never seen before (while I was connected with wallet A). The new list has 45 entries. Now I can again reload the list as often as I want, the list stays the same. But when I switch back to wallet A, the list is as before (42 fleets). WTF? So this means: I can´t see all fleets that are available to borrow. 
Question: Is this working as intended or is this a bug?
Correction: The list depends on the current faction. lol, that explains it  Thanks @Suto

I can see very strange RPC bursts in the network tab, up to 80 req/s, but I already reported this problem in my ticket. I also get rate limited by the IronForge RPC due to the RPC bursts of the rental page.

author: ROME|King Bryan-Titan Analytics [STAR]
content:
Heyo! 
I wanted to offer the team a massive congratulations on the release of ship rentals.  From my perspective it has been the best release yet.  It literally just worked and it wasn't even released at 23:59 Hawaii time.  And it's been a pretty huge success so far from what I've seen.  A TITAN was rented for 120k atlas per day. Epic.  And I'm also hearing stories from a lot of players who have managed to rent fleets at great deals and are flying them even now.  Seriously, well done.

Something that makes this release even more special in my eyes is that it's another tick off that "giant web3 space mmo" box.  This is another chapter in Star Atlas's legacy.  A stepping stone to the next release.  A foundation block that unlocks the rest of the structure. And with every step we get closer to a complete product.  It's just so damn exciting.

The Cyber Nation is growing ever closer.  And I'm so glad to be put part of it.

Thank you.    

Special shout out to @Cain and @[SLY] anthonyra for putting together this banger of a release.

author: XeanorONI
content:
Worth mentioning: in v3.1.117 we've added a trigger when Labs starts to initiate a refresh of your on-chain data. So in case something of a fleet didn't properly update after doing a transaction, this will probably fix that most of the times. If it doesn't, we'll need to know because that means the code that's supposed to update it still contains a bug somehow. "Please give starcomm a kick" should be a thing of the past.

So also in case of RPC outages, Solana going down, or anything similar outside of our control, it's fairly simple now to get back up to speed once those systems go back to functioning rather than having to wait for ATMTA re-dumping data or restarting systems.

P.S. Starcomm has also been removed from the marketplace on play.staratlas.com. It now uses firestore as well. You'll probably notice that the order book for an item will probably always load rather than staying empty sometimes.

author: XeanorONI
content: So does Starcomm V2 integrate with Firestore?  How does Starcomm V2 work with Ironforge?  How does Starframe fit into Ironforge, Starcomm V2 and Firestore?  Just trying to make sure I understand how all this amazing tech works together.

reply.preview: So does Starcomm V2 integrate with Firestore? How does Starcomm V2 work with Ironforge? How does Starframe fit into Ironforge, Starcomm V2 and Firestore? Just trying to make sure I understand how all this amazing tech works together.

author: [AEP] Mandalorian (Djin Djarin)
content: We have software that listens to all transactions and parses them and saves the data in Google Firestore if needed (for example, you create a fleet)  Sage Labs uses their interface to subscribe to that data in firestore, so if the data changes, that updated data is sent to Labs almost instantly. It’s how we scale up instead of hammering rpcs.  Starcomm v1 was a similar principle but the data was kept in memory across multiple machines.

reply.preview: We have software that listens to all transactions and parses them and saves the data in Google Firestore if needed (for example, you create a fleet) Sage Labs uses their interface to subscribe to that data in firestore, so if the data changes, that updated data is sent to Labs almost instantly. It’s how we scale up instead of hammering rpcs. Starcomm v1 was a similar principle but the data was kept in memory across multiple machines.

author: Xap✨ [STAR]
content: in sage there are updates to scanning patterns coming with the C4 update and the council rank perks allow for deeper specialization and access to different patterns through leveling up. we'll also be experimenting with different types of discoveries based on access to different scanning patterns and stronger scanning builds will also be more configurable when ship config comes online. we should should do a gut check on scanning/data runner ships as they should be the best at finding stuff even now, but can't control for everything economically all the time i suppose.   in ue5, my plan was to have a mini game where players can scan broad or narrow which effects range and penetration. so you can do a broad scan and get a short range and narrow for further, but with narrow you have to point your cone in a specific direction. and the tiers of components and hull specializations factor into how those perform. for range and penetration are also different stats that can be blended per component. and depth and potentially frequencies can lead to different discoveries on the same planet or system when two different tiers do the same scan from the same location. then sdus at some point i'd like a zk type thing where the goods can be evaluated for sale but don't reveal specific location details so players can actually have sdus at different value tiers but don't give up their cards. that relies on an offchain system or a zero knowledge system. scanners would also penetrate stealth tiers and detect different energy output schemes, gathering fleet movements as valuable information. also just superficial scanning of celestial bodies could have value in being the latest information uploaded to the star atlas. in fleet battles or dogfighting, deeper scanning capabilites could form subtargets on components to pass to your turret gunner to hit the power supply or engine for example to cripple a ship instead of trying to broadly damage the entire thing. a faster more skilled scanner could gain a competitive advantage.   I think data running with claim staking (not developing the claim stakes) could be one of the most engaging exploration loops. Finding a really valuable plot out in deep space and staking it for ownership then selling that to people who actually want to do the drilling will be a fun loop.

author: Xap✨ [STAR]
content: Yes that's all correct. Ultimately they'll be working of the same onchain programs and they're just different clients that interact with the programs. Each of the plots will be able to be identifiable onchain with their own ID and coordinates and we're syncing to make sure those stay aligned for the long term so they stay 1:1. And then for example in unreal you'll be able to station yourself into one of the HUBs in your claim and do some ground extraction and processing to contribute to your own local stash or to the claim's local storage. Which is identified in SAGE/onchain.   And deep space scanning applies to mostly planets but there are other things I'm planning for. Asteroids yes, I'm hoping we have big enough ones that you'll be able to inject an IMP and just have it extracting there for a while. Then nebulae for gas, using space stations for extraction and processing instead of buildings. Scanning even some empty space that could make space habs valuable locations as Chip mentioned with legrange points being an interesting perk to have. Also another mechanic that I'm still withholding until it passes the sniff test but an in lore way to make anomalies appear that can only be found by high tier scanners/data runners. I think it'll work great but don't want to go into detail because it spoils some upcoming story elements.   Also, I did mean to mention the XPL drones are also part of the scanning loop where you can send off different scanner drones with routing to spread out your data capture.

author: Xap✨ [STAR]
content: It's going to be a tricky one to balance because it makes no logical sense to be able to extract more raw resources mining solo. But there are other things besides volume to give an advantage such as xp earned that allows other perks to be upgraded faster. Or unreal solo discoverable minerals only. For pre-season progression we have a concept of a resource that can only be discovered in unreal solo that will be a part of onchain crafting recipes and output would be useful in SAGE. So, with that crossover, everyone would probably want to spend some time playing in unreal game modes or at least make it valuable for those who do by purchasing from them. But yes, this is something we'll want to balance over time. My philosophy has always been, anyone doing any career solo for about an hour should have the same general earn  rate. Of course if someone with equal skill also multiplies their fleet commands then they should be able multiply that with some automation trade offs like thinner margins.

author: [AEP] Mandalorian (Djin Djarin)
content: if we have the migration on launch there's probably a few ways it can go down so until we have that locked in then i can't say for certain. for example, the new Council Rank system unlocks perks by spending Renown. You earn XP and each level CR you gain you get Renown to spend on perks, such as fleet size. So, there might be a migration system where you get a conversion of the xp earned so far to a rank with a certain amount of Renown that you then spend how you see fit. Perhaps fleet size is all you care about then boom you're back to your proper fleet sizes capability. Still stuff to work through but a couple stepping stones until we can lock it down.

@lu

reply.preview: Can I ask why is it possible to craft ships just in the css? There's a specific reason for it? is penalizing for a player like me that lives in the mrz's, but mostly I'm genuinely curious about the specific reason, if is connected to the economy somehow. Thank you in advance

author: 《VΛ》ValPhoenix [STAR]
content: The reason is technical, ships as cargo can only become ships in game by being exported as cargo then imported as ships. Ships transitioning from cargo to be real ships adds a lot of interaction with the ship banks. The more spots where the banks are touched the more risk of a bug causing major issues.  We've been working on ways to do this in a way that satisfies our strict security requirements.

author: 𝐋ᴀɴᴢᴇʀ [SPS]
content: https://star-atlas-dao.gitbook.io/knowledge-base/overview/treasury  This might help.  The only current expenses are the Foundation fees which Swagner mentioned.  Those include corporate formalities + director fees.  The director fees were waived for the first 2 years. This is the first year they have been picked up.  The Council was debating how to pay for certain items like gitbooks  or other miscellaneous admin items and decided we could pull from the foundations 100k. However none of those expenses have materialized yet as we are still waiting on some basics like email accounts. For example, the knowledge base is still under FCs account.  @[AEP] Funcracker did you pay or get reimbursed yet?  The Council or Foundation has yet to make any payments for pips. Gamescom is the first pip that has required payment. And we still have to sort that out.   The Foundation and DAO is still severely under resourced so a lot of duties have been delegated back to ATMTA. E g. They still control the multi sigs with the treasury and Dan is doing double legal duty for the foundation, DAO, and ATMTA.   Because the reality is if the DAO and Foundation were to hire an actual team right now the treasury would not be sustainable.  It's not super well known but ATMTA does an absolute massive amount of work for the DAO unpaid and uncompensated.   Which makes sense in the fledgling state.    One of our goals in the council the first term was to take as many items off ATMTAs plate as possible.  For two major reasons.     First, because the DAO has to take the first step before we can take the second. Independence is not a sudden or immediate process.  At least not in the current funding environment.    Second.  If ATMTA employees are double booked, Star Atlas comes first.  Or SA employees are working nights and weekends to help the DAO out. Neither is desirable for the DAO.  The Council (and DAO) recognizes the game is the priority, so whatever we can do to prioritize that, we will.  Tying this up... The current Treasury documents may reflect the actual cash going out but not necessarily the time and effort being extended because ATMTA is not rebilling the DAO.

content:
Hey @bunthius, what’s up? I hope I’m not bothering you!

I was thinking about the future cargo limits we’ll have on Starbases, and I was wondering: assuming a player exceeds that limit when the update goes live, what will happen to the excess?
I was imagining maybe a completely new program and new map, or perhaps a system where a player can go over the limit temporarily—but once their resources drop below the threshold, they wouldn’t be able to go over it again? I know we are not so close to that update, just out of curiosity!

I’m not sure if my question is well phrased, but I hope you get what I mean!
Thanks so much for your time!

Told Grok to elaborate on my dream and this is what it wrote. Does anyone else miss these days? Can't wait!

Last night, I was swept away by a vivid, breathtaking dream—jerked awake in the shadowed stillness to the clarion call of battle, thrust into the chaos of a daring night raid. With heart-pounding adrenaline surging like wildfire through my veins, I rallied fleet command, orchestrating a symphony of strategy and valor under a star-strewn sky. That electric rush reignited the embers of those electrifying gaming days—moments of pure, unbridled joy I’ve yearned for with every fiber of my being. The anticipation swells within me, a tidal wave of hope and excitement, as I dream of that triumphant day when games rise once more to their rightful glory. Soon, we’ll soar again into the boundless expanse of epic adventures, forging legends among the cosmos, together unstoppable! #staratlas @Deleted User @bunthius @SW4GN3R

content:
@SW4GN3R , @bunthius and @Deleted User Hi, good morning, I have some questions about the DAO/DAC system (I never know which one is right here).
1 - Is there a plan to include a Guild/DAC/Group mode so players can organize themselves?
2 - Will this system allow for "direct" or "internal" markets where members can place values ​​outside the local market standard, but only group members will be able to see and buy these items?
3 - Will it be possible to include some kind of monthly fee/tax on DAC members?
4 - This is not a question, but a suggestion: if possible, at launch, include a filter to view members on the map without seeing other players AND an internal position system with structure management.
